title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was fatally shot after an altercation at a Lowell apartment complex on Pawtucket Boulevard.
The shooting involved two armed men who knew each other, with one man pronounced dead at a local hospital.
The Middlesex DA’s Office and Lowell Police are actively investigating; the second man remained at the scene.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston in 2027 after a purchase by the Houston Rockets' Fertitta family.
The team will be renamed the Houston Comets, reviving the city's former WNBA team disbanded in 2008.
The franchise was sold for a WNBA-record $300 million, pending relocation after the 2026 season.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Daredevil: Born Again' Season 2 is now available on Disney+, featuring Charlie Cox and Vincent D'Onofrio.
'From the World of John Wick: Ballerina' stars Ana de Armas and Lance Reddick, streaming on HBO Max.
'Bait', a new series with Riz Ahmed, is streaming on Prime Video, showcasing a comedic take on the James Bond franchise.

title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown is out against the Hawks with left Achilles tendinitis, as announced by Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla.
Brown recorded 31 points against Oklahoma City Thunder in his last game, contributing to a 119-109 victory for Boston.
Celtics are 48-24 and hold second place in the Eastern Conference as the playoff race intensifies.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington ban gas-powered leaf blowers due to noise and emissions concerns.
73% of over 250 Boston.com poll respondents oppose the bans, citing practicality issues with electric alternatives.
Supporters of the bans cite noise, fumes, and health concerns; some consider gas blowers outdated technology.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The BA.3.2 variant, nicknamed 'cicada,' was detected in Massachusetts' wastewater and originated in South Africa in 2024.
It evades antibodies from recent vaccines, although it's not shown to cause increased hospitalizations or deaths, according to WHO.
Current vaccines continue to provide protection against severe disease despite BA.3.2's presence in 23 countries and multiple U.S. states.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge allowed a lawsuit to proceed against two DCF employees for removing children in Waltham.
The lawsuit alleges DCF workers ordered the children's seizure without parental consent or a warrant.
Judge removed Waltham police and other DCF employees from the lawsuit, focusing on two main defendants.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Willie's on Beacon Hill by Chef Jamie Bissonnette offers global-influenced pizzas and pastas, now open in Boston.
Ladyfish in Portland, by Chef Jordan Rubin, serves Maine seafood with Chinese and Italian influences, opens May 1.
La Tavernetta by Chef Douglass Williams will open in East Boston, featuring oysters and spritzes, details forthcoming.

title: The Boston Public Library is the star of Kate Quinn’s latest NYT bestseller
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Kate Quinn's novel 'The Astral Library' features the Boston Public Library as a magical portal.
'The Astral Library' debuted at No. 2 on the New York Times bestsellers list.
Quinn, a Boston University alum, combines her love for Boston with fantasy elements in her latest book.

title: Councilors, Pressley doubling down on demands for bodycam footage after deadly police shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston City Council adopts resolution urging timely release of police bodycam footage after Roxbury shooting incident.
Officer Nicholas O'Malley faces manslaughter charges in Stephenson King's death; bodycam footage central in investigation.
U.S. Rep. Ayanna Pressley joins calls for footage release, emphasizing transparency for the involved families' healing.
